##NAMES=Woodhouseite
##RRUFFID=R060520
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CaAl_3_(SO_4_)(PO_4_)(OH)_6_
##LOCALITY=Champion mine, Mono County, California, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Michael Scott
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless cubes, with striations that display the rhombohedral symmetry
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R060520
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_0.98_Sr_0.02_)Al_3.00_[(P_0.53_S_0.47_)O_4_]_2_((OH)_5.94_(H_2_O)_0.06_); = darker phase  (Ca_0.94_Sr_0.06_)Al_3.00_[(P_0.52_S_0.48_)O_4_]_2_((OH)_5.96_(H_2_O)_0.04_)= lighter phase
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 6.7507 b: 6.7507 c: 6.7507 alpha: 62.265 beta: 62.265 gamma: 62.265 volume: 228.54 crystal system: hexagonal
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
